description: Family letter from Yosef to Abū l-Ḥasan (his brother-in-law?). In Judaeo-Arabic, with the address in Arabic script. Dating: 13th–15th century. The sender defends himself against blame he received from the addressee. The sender plans to come for Ḥannuka. "I heard that my sister is coming, but don't let her stir from her bed, whatever happens with (her plan of?) coming." Greetings to various people, including the same sister, to whom Umm Najīb also sends her regards. Greetings to Abū l-Najm. (Information in part from Mediterranean Society, v, p. 395 and from Goitein's hand list.) AA/ASE.
